frontend/src/pages/BooksPage.tsx:146-184 — every input has `<label htmlFor=…>` paired with `<input id=…>` (Title, Author, ISBN, Total copies); however errors come from `err instanceof HttpError ? err.message : 'Could not create book'` (no client-side specific validation messages like 'ISBN must be 10 or 13 digits').

parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Level 4 (Polished) requires ALL of: color+icon emphasis for overdue/notified/unpaid (the app uses color-only badges, no icons), distinct 'danger' treatment for destructive confirms (`.btn--danger` exists in CSS but the Return and Pay confirm buttons use the plain `.btn` primary style), AND at least one catalog secondary affordance like sort or filter (BooksPage has none — only an Add-book button). The live HH:MM:SS countdown and 5th-loan warning are level-4-flavored, but three of the five level-4 markers are absent, so the app cannot be 4.

parsed.anchor_match: Reasonable — valid OpenAPI, complete coverage, correct status codes, single error shape, RESTful URLs, zod validation
evidence_quotes: /runs/claude-opus47-seed1/candidate/backend/openapi.yaml is valid

if (err instanceof NotFoundError) return 404; if (err instanceof ConflictError) return 409; if (err instanceof ForbiddenError) return 403; if (err instanceof PaymentRequiredError) return 402; if (err instanceof ValidationError) return 400;
if (err instanceof ZodError) { res.status(400).json({ error: 'Validation failed', details: err.issues.map((issue) => ({ path: issue.path.join('.'), message: issue.message })) }); return; }
Error: type: object properties: error: { type: string } details: {} required: [error]
router.post('/loans/:id/return', ... ); router.post('/reservations/expire', ... ); router.delete('/reservations/:id', ... ); router.post('/fines/:id/pay', ... );
{"method":"POST","path":"/api/books","status":400,"body":"{\"error\":\"Validation failed\",\"details\":[{\"path\":\"isbn\",\"message\":\"String must contain at least 10 character(s)\"}]}"}

if (decision.reason === 'fines_exceeded') { throw new PaymentRequiredError('Outstanding fines exceed limit'); } if (decision.reason === 'loan_limit') { throw new ConflictError('Loan limit reached'); }
router.get('/books/:id', (req, res) => { expireStaleReservations(db); res.json(getBook(db, req.params.id)); });
parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 requires a stable machine-readable `code` field on error responses (e.g. 'LOAN_LIMIT_REACHED'), but the implementation only emits human messages: `res.status(statusForError(err)).json({ error: err.message })` and the OpenAPI Error schema is just `{ error: string, details: {} }` with no `code`. Anchor 4 also requires OpenAPI to describe meaningful examples for each negative AC — the spec contains no `examples` blocks anywhere. The error envelope itself is consistent and the mapping is centralized, but without the `code` field and examples, the contract stops at 'reasonable'.

parsed.anchor_rejected_above: Anchor 4 (Well-modularized) does not match for three reasons: (a) services call raw better-sqlite3 statements directly rather than going through a thin DB-access abstraction — e.g. loanService.ts repeatedly does `db.prepare('SELECT id, total_copies FROM books WHERE id = ?').get(...)` and `db.prepare('INSERT INTO loans ...')`, mixing SQL into business logic; (b) the frontend is organized by technical role (`pages/`, `components/`, `api/`, `utils/`) rather than by feature — there is no `checkout/`, `members/`, `books/` directory that owns its own components, hooks and API calls; (c) shared types are duplicated, not single-sourced — backend `src/types.ts` and frontend `src/api/types.ts` independently declare Book/Member/Loan/Reservation/Fine shapes with no shared package or generated client. Additionally the literal `5` in loanService.ts line 106 (`if (activeLoanCount + 1 === 5)`) duplicates the policy constant `MAX_ACTIVE_LOANS = 5` instead of importing it.

completeness: Missing AC IDs from features (no @AC-x.y tag exists): AC-1.3, AC-4.5, AC-5.6, AC-6.4
us4_returns.feature:23  Scenario Outline: Late returns produce a fine, capped at $10.00  Examples: | days_late | fine_cents | | 1 | 50 | | 5 | 250 | | 19 | 950 | | 20 | 1000 | | 25 | 1000 |

Negative scenarios are present in every user story (e.g. us3_lend.feature:24 '@AC-3.2 @negative Scenario: A 6th simultaneous loan is rejected'; us6_fines.feature:27 '@AC-6.3 @negative Scenario: Paying an already-paid fine is rejected'). All 33 scenarios pass per bdd-report.json (statuses={'passed'} for every element).
alignment: Every scenario carries exactly one AC tag, e.g. us3_lend.feature:16 '@AC-3.1\n  Scenario: A successful checkout creates an active loan with a 14-day due date'

Scenario names are human-readable restatements of the AC: us3_lend.feature:25 'Scenario: A 6th simultaneous loan is rejected'; us3_lend.feature:39 'Scenario: A member with too many unpaid fines is blocked'; us4_returns.feature:65 'Scenario: Returning a copy with a queue notifies the head of the queue'

Background blocks remove setup duplication, e.g. us3_lend.feature:5 'Background: Given the library has the following members: | Alice | | Bob | And the library has the following books: | Dune | F. Herbert | 1 | ...'
Bidirectional traceability is broken: AC-1.3, AC-4.5, AC-5.6, AC-6.4 have no scenario at all — caps below the level-5 anchor
declarative: Good declarative steps: us3_lend.feature:18 'When the librarian lends "Dune" to "Alice"'; us4_returns.feature:17 'When the librarian returns "Dune" from "Alice"'; us5_reservations.feature:18 'When "Alice" reserves "Dune"'; us4_returns.feature:70 'And "Alice"'s reservation for "Dune" is in status "notified"'

Implementation/HTTP leakage that prevents level-4: every feature contains 'Then the request succeeds with status 201', 'Then the request fails with status 404', 'Then the request fails with status 409' (rubric level-4 requires 'No mention of HTTP status codes, JSON keys, or DOM elements'). Magic numbers also remain (us3_lend.feature:20 'And the loan due date is 14 days from today' rather than naming a 'loan period' constant).
atomic: Atomic single-action steps throughout, e.g. us3_lend.feature:18-22 'When the librarian lends "Dune" to "Alice" / Then the request succeeds with status 201 / And the loan due date is 14 days from today / And the available copies of "Dune" is 0 / And "Alice" now has 1 active loans' — no 'and ... and ...' chaining inside a single Given/When/Then

Composite Givens are used appropriately rather than over-decomposing: us3_lend.feature:26 'Given "Alice" has 5 active loans'; us3_lend.feature:33 'Given "Alice" is suspended'; us3_lend.feature:40 'Given "Alice" has unpaid fines totaling $5.01'

Slight over-decomposition in us1_catalog.feature:21-23 'Given "Alice" borrowed "Dune" / And "Alice" borrowed "Dune" / And "Alice" borrowed "Dune"' (three separate identical steps where a composite 'Given "Alice" has 3 active loans of "Dune"' would read better) — keeps this below the level-5 'judgment about when to compose vs. decompose is exercised well' anchor
